publication_metadata.title: The Apologetic Authority: A Structural Critique of Anthropic's Constitution for Claude | Zenodo
publication_metadata.description: The Apologetic Authority is a structural critique of Anthropic’s Constitution for Claude. It examines constitutional AI as a governance architecture, with attention to authority delegation, aspirational language, opacity, auditability, model self-description, institutional accountability, and the limits of constitutional framing as a safety mechanism. The manuscript argues that constitutional language can create an apologetic authority structure: a system in which the appearance of principled constraint may obscure unresolved questions of authorship, enforcement, revision, responsibility, and evidentiary traceability. This record archives version v1.0.1 of the manuscript and links it to the canonical web publication surface. Its evidentiary status is an independent AI governance and model-behavior analysis. Journal publication, institutional reporting, product release, and official Anthropic assessment remain outside the record’s claim boundary.
